title: SSRF, DNS Rebinding, Injection, Response Validation, and Least Authority · Browser and Node C… · Type-System Trace: Ssrf Destination Policy
summary: A server-rendering image proxy and browser preview must address SSRF, DNS rebinding, CORS limits, hostile media types, and privacy-safe diagnostics. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts ssrf destination policy increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch defense-in-depth outbound request policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Allow-list required schemes, ports, hosts, and destinations and enforce network egress limits for server-side fetching. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Validate environment, file, runtime, and secret-provider configuration with precedence, rotation, and safe diagnostics. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
